kills: C2 is vacuous as stated, provable by hand: S_k symmetry + leg marginals force B(theta) = b1 I + b2 (J - I) with b1 + (k-1)b2 = 1/k, the bridge forces k b1 = p(2 sin(theta/2)), and Graf gives hat[p(2 sin(theta/2))](n) = integral J_n(lambda)^2 dmu >= 0 automatically (n=0 case covered by the DC atom >= 1/k). Every spin-block constraint is implied by the abelian triangle LP constraints, so the C2 SDP equals the e3c/L9 LP (value 3.72), k=4 is FEASIBLE, and the conjecture dies without running anything. Root cause: summing B over the apex color c0 reduces it to the pure 2-point correlation; the proposal's own Graf identity proves its own redundancy.

C3's cheap sanity check is false: hat B(1) = integral J_1(lambda)^2 dmu > 0 for ANY proper coloring (p(0)=1, p(1)=0 forces spectral mass off lambda=0), so the Isbell 7-coloring is NOT achiral under the stated definition, and the achiral sector excludes every proper coloring at every k. The predicted achiral-vs-unrestricted feasibility gap is trivially present and certifies nothing about 4 vs 5.

C3 category error: odd spin n is parity under rotation by pi (orientation-preserving), not chirality; mirror reflection acts by n -> -n. B(theta) depends only on chord length 2 sin(theta/2), hence is automatically even, hence hat B(n) = hat B(-n) for every coloring: chirality is structurally inexpressible at the B level. At the apex-colored level, mirror asymmetry would be y_{c0,i,j} != y_{c0,j,i}, but the imposed S_k symmetrization forces equality via the transposition (i j). The (1,1,d) triangle and the rhombus are both mirror-symmetric configurations, so no order the proposal reaches can see chirality at all.

C3 convexity kill (half-admitted in the proposal): all constraints are linear in moments and reflection-equivariant, so reflection-averaging any feasible point is WLOG; a convex moment hierarchy can never certify that chirality is NECESSARY. Only rank-constrained or exact moment formulations could, and none is proposed.

C4's tractability claim is unsubstantiated: the rhombus family is a 1-parameter slice, but genuine order-2 PSD closure requires 4-point moments over the full 3-parameter relative-motion moduli of two colored unit segments, plus localization (nonnegativity, properness) constraints that live on the moduli and do not block-diagonalize. Either the blocks are again apex-summed/vacuous (inheriting the C2 collapse) or the instance is comparable to, not orders of magnitude smaller than, the 48342-variable X_23 Lasserre.

C4 internal inconsistency: the 4-point Moser rhombus (both centers plus both intersection points) has D_2 mirror symmetry and is achiral; the chiral object is the labeled triple with ONE intersection point chosen. The 'minimal configuration carrying chirality' framing contradicts C3's own geometry.

C4 violates the no-buffer prior: the abelian ladder went 3.483 (L6) -> 3.72 (e3c triangle) -> 3.82 (e3e Moser) -> 4.05 (Ambrus et al. 2023, the current world record, via heavy LP with finite configurations). C4 claims one near-LP-scale order jumps past 5 to certify chi_m >= 6. Every prior step of that ladder cost a strictly richer structural input.

C2's rhetoric 'purely harmonic-analytic with no finite-subgraph input' is false on its face: the theta = pi/3 properness zero IS the K_3 subgraph constraint, and config-augmented LP proofs of chi_m >= 5 already exist (Ambrus et al. 2023), so even the repaired version re-proves a theorem twice over (Falconer 1981 classically, Ambrus computationally).

C1's novelty is thin: in SDP form group-averaging is WLOG by convexity and linearity, and the radial reduction is standard BNOV 2009 / Oliveira-Vallentin 2010 practice; the Lovasz 1979 edge-transitive argument is the finite shadow. Its heuristic proof sketch is incomplete (spin channels can raise the symbol at secondary J_0 maxima; the sandwich needs essential-spectrum care), so it remains a conjecture, though my counterexample probes (degree <= 2 spin mixtures) found nothing beating ratio 2.4829.

Verification-protocol flaw (minor): 'dual certificate re-verified on a 10x finer grid' is not a proof; the dual condition is a univariate Bessel inequality on all lambda > 0 and must be closed with interval arithmetic plus tail asymptotics (doable with mpmath, but the protocol as written would not constitute a certificate).
prior_art_collisions: Lovasz 1979: ratio/Hoffman bound equals theta for vertex- and edge-transitive graphs; the finite shadow of C1.
Bachoc-Nebe-Oliveira-Vallentin 2009 and Oliveira-Vallentin 2010: the radial (J_0) reduction and the 0.287119 / 3.4829 plane values; makes C1 folklore-adjacent, as the proposal admits.

DeCorte-Oliveira-Vallentin completely-positive hierarchy and de Laat-Vallentin k-point bounds: the repaired per-apex-color spin-block SDP is an instance of known 3-point machinery; the SE(2)-irrep presentation is cosmetic unless it demonstrably changes tractability.

Bachoc-Passuello-Thiery 2015 (m_1 <= 0.2588 via simplex constraints) and Ambrus-Csiszarik-Matolcsi-Varga-Zsamboki 2023 (m_1 <= 0.2470, chi_m >= 5): the LP-with-configurations route already crossed 4, deflating C2's headline; repo e3i/e3g reproduce this lineage.
Falconer 1981: the theorem C2 targets is 45 years old; only the method would be new.
Graf addition theorem (Watson; SE(2) harmonic analysis, Vilenkin): standard identity, correctly stated in the proposal, and it is exactly what proves the C2 spin blocks redundant.

Repo internal: L6 (3.4829 baseline), L9/e3c (3.72 triangle ceiling), e3e (3.82 with Moser translations), L43 (order-1 relaxations provably too weak), L44-area X_23 blocker (98627 -> 48342 variables after congruence reduction), all confirmed in experiments/LEARNINGS.md and experiments/fractional/e3b_ofv_bessel_lp.py, e3c_ofv_lp_dual.py.
result.surviving_kernel: Three things survive. (1) C1 as a localization lemma: all 2-point SE(2)-covariant spectral content for the plane collapses to the J_0 compression, so any spectral gain must be genuinely >= 3-point with the apex color retained; plausible, folklore-adjacent, worth a short rigorous proof. (2) The obvious repair of C2: impose matrix-Herglotz spin blocks PER APEX COLOR, hat[y_{c0,.,.}](n) PSD for each c0 (do not sum over c0). This is the color-conditioned circle autocorrelation around an apex, a genuinely 3-point constraint NOT implied by Graf from the 2-point sector, and after S_k reduction it is still ~2 scalar channels per n (near-LP scale, runnable on this stack today). Whether it moves the bound past 3.72 is open and cheap to decide. (3) The strategic idea behind C4: the finite graph X_23 breaks O(2) and pays in variables, while the continuum keeps O(2) exact, so an SE(2)-Plancherel (operator-Bochner) formulation of the order-2 relaxation is a legitimate route around the blocker-5 solver wall; but it must be formulated on the full relative-motion moduli of pairs of colored unit segments, not the rhombus slice.
result.cheapest_decisive_test: Two-stage, both trivial on this stack. Stage 0 (zero solver cost, ~20 lines of sympy): verify the collapse identity, i.e. that under S_k symmetry + leg marginals + bridge, the eigenchannels of hat B(n) are (1/k) delta_{n0} and (hat[p(2 sin(theta/2))](n) - delta_{n0}/k)/(k-1) with hat[p(2 sin(theta/2))](n) = integral J_n(lambda)^2 dmu; this confirms C2-as-stated equals the e3c LP and kills it on paper. Stage 1 (the decisive run, minutes of Clarabel/HiGHS time): implement the repaired instance with per-apex-color blocks hat[y_{c0}](n) PSD, k = 4, N = 8, theta grid 720, lambda grid 160; if the optimal bound stays at 3.72 the SE(2) spin route is dead at triangle order and C4 should be abandoned; if it moves materially above 3.73 the kernel has real content and escalation to a properly formulated 4-point order is justified.
result.steelman: The strongest defensible version of this proposal: (i) Prove C1 rigorously as a negative localization theorem, 'every SE(2)-covariant 2-point spectral bound for chi_m(R^2) is capped at 1 + 1/0.402759', via the convex-averaging argument at the SDP level plus a continuity argument for essential spectra; this converts the Gelfand-pair structure into a wall like L55's monoid and justifies spending all effort at >= 3 points. (ii) Replace the apex-summed blocks with per-apex-color matrix-Herglotz blocks hat[y_{c0}](n) PSD: this is the minimal genuinely noncommutative 3-point constraint, it sits strictly between the e3c abelian triangle LP and the DOV 3-point hierarchy, and its S_k reduction keeps the instance near-LP. Run it at k = 4 as the decisive cheap bet; the honest expectation, calibrated by the 3.48 -> 3.72 -> 3.82 -> 4.05 ladder, is a modest gain over 3.72, not a crossing of 4. (iii) If and only if (ii) shows a real gain, formulate the true continuum order-2 relaxation on the 3-parameter moduli of pairs of colored unit segments with SE(2)-Plancherel operator blocks per (lambda, spin window), as an O(2)-exact alternative to the blocked X_23 Lasserre; target an incremental record past 4.05 toward 5, with chi_m >= 6 as a long-shot ceiling, and state explicitly that any success bounds chi_m, not chi. (iv) Drop chirality entirely as a convex-hierarchy claim; if pursued at all, reformulate it as a statement about exact moment sets of actual 5-colorings on scalene or generic 4-point configurations, where mirror asymmetry is first expressible, and note that S_k symmetrization must be relaxed to see it.
